tokens: You're about 7 minutes behind right now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob's comment about being 7 minutes down) What!?
(Pause) I haven't quite learned how to pick up rocks in my hands yet, Bob, or I would've had you a sample.
It's an old blue traverse gravimeter!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'd like to cut through here, with a T-38, sometime.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know, you just got to take it easy until you learn to work in one-sixth g.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I haven't learned to pick up rocks, which is a very embarrassing thing for a geologist.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I look like an elephant stumbling around here.
(Pause) (Talking to himself) Careful with the LCRU.
Careful with this baby (because of the relatively fragile electronics).
(Pause) That's the real one (rather than a training version).
(To Jack, who is mounting the geopallet on the back of the Rover) Boy, you sure move that Rover around when you do that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm getting pretty good at throwing things.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, that thing (a connector on the Television Control Unit or TCU) won't want to go on.
That's because it's not in there.
Put it in right, and it goes on.
Okay, the power cable's on the TCU, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I wish you'd be more careful.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's warm out here, you know?
I'm certainly glad I got cooling.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, the TCU is locked in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, I've seen an awful lot of rocks, as I work here.
They look just like those pyroxene gabbros that I mentioned.
The pyroxene's iridescent in the bright Sun.
The grain size is about, oh, between...Maybe the mean is 2 millimeters with max maybe up at 3 or 4.
And it looks like predominantly a pyroxene plagioclase rock...(correcting himself) clinopyroxene, but I haven't looked at it real closely.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll set the rake on the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just haven't learned...I'm getting more finesse now.
I think you can overwork yourself, instead of making use of the one-sixth gravity.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's going to take a whole EVA to get familiar.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I find I'm using my arms almost as much as I ever did.
I remember the last time I was on the Moon: about 2 hours ago.
That old hammer goes to the gate top.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm getting the low gain (antenna) out now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Referring to the Rover power-up) Felt like a Rover, huh, Geno?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I just couldn't feel it murmur when I pressed the breakers in.
I could life in it, but...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Fields impression) Hey, you let me down, sport.
There's a pin you didn't pull.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I'll let you get that; keep you honest.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Would you believe that the doggone (low-gain) antenna...Here; Jack, when I bend this, pull the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got to open it up to get it out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, connector is wedged in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing) Well, that's probably the way it was designed.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, don't drop any of those connectors on the...(Discarding something) Look at that go.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Good thing we're well-coordinated human beings.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, I can't believe...Yeah, I can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Which way are you going to put it on?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I thought maybe I would put it on that way; so I will put it on this way, because that's probably right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you put it on right, you're going to disappoint me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, I hate to touch...I touched the old gnomon!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll do my best to clean (it).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We copy the low-gain hooked up.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (As a fanfare, rising in pitch) Da, da-da doe!
The rake is on the extension handle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The scoop is on the extension handle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And your exuberance is showing up on the BTUs.
You're running a little high on those.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I've never been calmer in my life.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think it's a great deal a part to just get accustomed to handling yourself in zero gravity.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The only vise on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I thought you were at one-sixth g.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, you know where we are.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself) Okay, old sample bag...Sample containment bag...(correcting himself) Sample collection bag (number 2), or whatever, is going (on the gate) (Garbled) (Pause) (Singing) "What is this thing called crazy." Come on.
Some of the simplest things in the world you forget.
(Pause) Okay, let's get this right this time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You did a great job of parking (the Rover), so I was standing in a hole.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Don't want to mess up all those good looking craters around here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Most staffs are accessory, I've learned.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob the high-gain (TV antenna) is up and connected.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See if I can't get you a TV camera.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Ah, let's keep them in...(Listens to Bob)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'd like to stay on Intermediate, Bob.
I'm not cold but I'm pleasant.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You're fine, no problem; your option, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I just don't want to run out of consumables (oxygen and cooling water) about 6 or 7 hours (into the EVA).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't think it makes any difference.
You got to use (means "get rid of") the heat.
Matter of fact, that's one of the little known facts of this business, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got the TV camera in my hand, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You owe yourself 30 seconds to look up over the South Massif and look at the Earth!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You seen one Earth, you've seen them all.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: When you begin to believe that...Come on camera, go in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Apparently still mounting an SCB on the accessory staff) I'll look in a minute, Gene.
But I tell you, once I start this little operation, if I don't finish it, it never gets done.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Talking to the TV camera) Okay, get in there.
(Reading) TCU; sunshade to camera and then the cable.
Okay, let me get the sunshade.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's always more of a job than it ought to be.
However, SCB-3 is on the handhold.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I'm getting smarter about one-sixth g.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Snaps in, snaps closed with the slightest flick of a coordinated wrist.
Oh, it's over here (on the MESA).
(I can) just still barely see the
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Chuckling) We ought to get those, before we go hungry.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we'll get them (the scissors) when we get the tongs out, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There are some tongs in the Rover, and I'll come over and get them in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we refrained from mentioning that to Ron.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Tell him I hope he's enjoying our
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now let me see if I can find the beautiful big dot (that is, Earth) up there.
I know what I'm going to have to do.
I'm going to have to get the...Oh, I got it right there!
Might be able to peak that but I got that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Put my hand over it, so I could see it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about that piece of Velcro there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's just what I'm working on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The trouble is to reach it (the viewfinder), I've got to...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself) Okay, I'll bet you it (meaning the checklist) says put Mag Bravo (on the CDR camera)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, Earth is in the circle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'm going to close the (LCRU) circuit breaker, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me give you some numbers.
AGC (Automatic Gain Control) is about 3.4; temp is about 1.8; and power is about 2.1.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the TV is all yours...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Have you got a power reading there for us, Geno?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob) Okay, I'll give you a power reading, External, if you want it.
I'll give you...Temp is still about 1.7 and Power is about 1.8 on External.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
